title: Remove stray outbox writes and trim idle player readback churn
summary: 0.6.27 keeps the restored 0.6.24 core-player baseline and the 0.6.26 video-time-owned sync seam, but removes normal-build runtime event outbox appends and slows or change-gates player readback updates so idle and paused playback stop paying for test-surface baggage.
changes: Disable runtime event outbox file appends in the normal non-test core-player build so the player no longer writes session event JSONL during ordinary playback use.
Slow the shared rig or player readback refresh cadence from 0.20s to 1.00s for this lane.
Only write player target, media, state, parity, timeline, playlist, scrub, and volume storables when the visible value actually changes.
Stamp version 0.6.27 into player.version.json and BuildRuntimeInfo.cs.
Build and raw-deploy the 0.6.27 core-player alpha seam with package inventory generated via -SkipVarDistribute.
reasoning: The current lag report described worsening hourglass freezes even when playback was paused on images, which pointed away from the new video-time-owned sync seam and toward hidden background work.
Code review showed that the runtime still carried an event outbox append path and that player readback summaries were being rebuilt and reassigned on a frequent cadence even when they were unchanged.
This slice stays narrow on file-I/O hygiene and idle refresh churn only; it does not redesign the panel, reopen playlist policy, or change the sync authority model.
operatorVisibleOutcomes: The core player should stop appending runtime event outbox files during ordinary non-test playback.
Idle or paused playback should spend less time rebuilding hidden readback payloads and writing unchanged slider or status values.
knownIssues: Preset read and write behavior still exists intentionally for saved preset and playlist workflows.
Meta and innerpiece package-storage code paths still exist in the repo, but this slice targets the core-player runtime lane only.
